body_txt: Pick a war. Any war. Then write a story about it; it doesn’t matter if the story is fictional or Based on a True Story . It doesn’t matter if the war in question actually happened or not. The point is to write a story that is meant to entertain and, usually, to make a point. For obvious reasons, people from countries that participated in a war are more likely to write about them. Usually media will focus on one unit whether it's a regiment, a company, or something smaller, like a squad or platoon. If the media is live-action, then it is usually based on foot soldiers due the expensive nature of filming aerial, tank, and naval combat. There are exceptions that use CGI, models, and stock footage of course. This trope is named for the catchphrase of Uncle Albert from Only Fools and Horses . Note that the correct pronunciation is " JYEW-rin-na WAW-ah ". Contrast War Was Beginning and Great Offscreen War . See also Military and Warfare Tropes and Wars In History . Real-Life Wars Fictional Types of War
